cases.pattern_name: Shared infrastructure brushing and subsidy skimming
cases.assertion: AER-001 detects new-account subsidy skimming: shared devices/IPs/payment accounts, fast refunds, low-quality logistics, and weak normal-business counter explanations.
AER-002 detects reputation manipulation: repeated micro-orders, duplicate review text/images, automation signals, shared infrastructure, and merchant-level rating inflation.

cases.pattern_name: Promotion subsidy arbitrage and refund extraction
cases.assertion: AER-003 detects subsidy arbitrage: concentrated eligibility keys and payment accounts repeatedly extract high discounts with abnormal refund/logistics patterns.

observations.summary: Expanded entity-resolution graph across account, device, IP, payment, logistics, and evidence nodes.

support_evidence.description: 14 users and 80 orders share 3 devices; reuse ratio 26.7.
80 orders concentrate on 2 datacenter/proxy IP addresses.

nodes.label: EC-SKIM-001
nodes.text: AER-001 detects new-account subsidy skimming: shared devices/IPs/payment accounts, fast refunds, low-quality logistics, and weak normal-business counter explanations.

observations.summary: Refund cluster query compared case refunds with dynamic promotion cohort baseline.

support_evidence.description: 67 of 80 orders were refunded; lift is 14.0x cohort baseline and 34 refunds occurred within 24h.

observations.summary: Logistics trace query assessed shipment authenticity and possible warehouse-batch counter explanations.

support_evidence.description: 80 logistics records have low cost/low trace quality and 80 auto-confirm receipts.

observations.summary: Counter-evidence search tested normal-business explanations before passport emission.

counter_evidence.description: No strong old-customer, campus, household, organic campaign, or warehouse-batch counter-evidence was found.

passport.risk_assertion: AER-001 detects new-account subsidy skimming: shared devices/IPs/payment accounts, fast refunds, low-quality logistics, and weak normal-business counter explanations.

support_evidence.description: 14 users and 80 orders share 3 devices; reuse ratio 26.7.
80 orders concentrate on 2 datacenter/proxy IP addresses.
80 logistics records have low cost/low trace quality and 80 auto-confirm receipts.
67 of 80 orders were refunded; lift is 14.0x cohort baseline and 34 refunds occurred within 24h.

counter_evidence.description: No strong old-customer, campus, household, organic campaign, or warehouse-batch counter-evidence was found.

remaining_uncertainty: Payment account ownership still requires review.
Warehouse batch shipping is only partially ruled out.

human_gate.reason: Evidence quality and disposition require auditor review.

passport.model_narrative: The case has multi-source support evidence and limited counter-evidence. Human review is required before disposition.

observations.summary: Behavior-sequence analysis fused uploaded gateway logs, device logs, and review content similarity.

support_evidence.description: Uploaded device/gateway logs show average automation score 0.79 across 64 device events.
64 comments include 128 duplicate text/image reuse hits.

observations.summary: Expanded entity-resolution graph across account, device, IP, payment, logistics, and evidence nodes.

support_evidence.description: 24 users and 64 orders share 4 devices; reuse ratio 16.0.
64 orders concentrate on 2 datacenter/proxy IP addresses.

nodes.label: EC-FAKE-002
nodes.text: AER-002 detects reputation manipulation: repeated micro-orders, duplicate review text/images, automation signals, shared infrastructure, and merchant-level rating inflation.

observations.summary: Case memory retrieval compared current evidence against approved historical pattern trajectories.

support_evidence.description: Historical memory 'Review farm with verified micro orders' matches this case pattern: Prior reputation manipulation case: low-value orders, duplicate review images, high automation score, and few refunds.

observations.summary: Counter-evidence search tested normal-business explanations before passport emission.

counter_evidence.description: No strong old-customer, campus, household, organic campaign, or warehouse-batch counter-evidence was found.

passport.risk_assertion: AER-002 detects reputation manipulation: repeated micro-orders, duplicate review text/images, automation signals, shared infrastructure, and merchant-level rating inflation.

support_evidence.description: Uploaded device/gateway logs show average automation score 0.79 across 64 device events.
24 users and 64 orders share 4 devices; reuse ratio 16.0.
64 orders concentrate on 2 datacenter/proxy IP addresses.

Historical memory 'Review farm with verified micro orders' matches this case pattern: Prior reputation manipulation case: low-value orders, duplicate review images, high automation score, and few refunds.
64 comments include 128 duplicate text/image reuse hits.

counter_evidence.description: No strong old-customer, campus, household, organic campaign, or warehouse-batch counter-evidence was found.

remaining_uncertainty: The lack of payment cluster evidence introduces uncertainty about the extent of financial involvement in the manipulation. Further investigation is needed to address this gap.
recommended_action: Query the payment cluster for transactions associated with the involved entities to assess the financial impact and confirm the presence of fraudulent activities.

human_gate.reason: Evidence quality and disposition require auditor review.

passport.model_narrative: The investigation into AER-002 has identified strong evidence of reputation manipulation through repeated micro-orders, duplicate reviews, and automated behavior. The case is marked as critical due to the high risk level and significant statistical support. However, there is a notable gap in payment cluster analysis, which could provide additional insights into potential financial fraud.

observations.summary: Subsidy ledger query linked promotion rules, eligibility keys, and repeated subsidy extraction.

support_evidence.description: 72 subsidy claims total 3027.84; repeated eligibility keys=15 and average subsidy=42.05.

observations.summary: Refund cluster query compared case refunds with dynamic promotion cohort baseline.

support_evidence.description: 23 of 72 orders were refunded; lift is 4.3x cohort baseline and 14 refunds occurred within 24h.

observations.summary: Expanded entity-resolution graph across account, device, IP, payment, logistics, and evidence nodes.

support_evidence.description: 18 users and 72 orders share 5 devices; reuse ratio 14.4.
72 orders concentrate on 3 datacenter/proxy IP addresses.

nodes.label: EC-ARBI-003
nodes.text: AER-003 detects subsidy arbitrage: concentrated eligibility keys and payment accounts repeatedly extract high discounts with abnormal refund/logistics patterns.

observations.summary: Counter-evidence search tested normal-business explanations before passport emission.

counter_evidence.description: No strong old-customer, campus, household, organic campaign, or warehouse-batch counter-evidence was found.

passport.risk_assertion: AER-003 detects subsidy arbitrage: concentrated eligibility keys and payment accounts repeatedly extract high discounts with abnormal refund/logistics patterns.

support_evidence.description: 18 users and 72 orders share 5 devices; reuse ratio 14.4.
72 orders concentrate on 3 datacenter/proxy IP addresses.
23 of 72 orders were refunded; lift is 4.3x cohort baseline and 14 refunds occurred within 24h.
72 subsidy claims total 3027.84; repeated eligibility keys=15 and average subsidy=42.05.

counter_evidence.description: No strong old-customer, campus, household, organic campaign, or warehouse-batch counter-evidence was found.

remaining_uncertainty: Logistics authenticity and specific promo cohort outliers
recommended_action: Expand investigation into logistics trace and compare promo cohorts to identify potential anomalies.

human_gate.reason: Evidence quality and disposition require auditor review.

passport.model_narrative: The case AER-003 involves a critical risk of promotion subsidy arbitrage and refund extraction. The assertion is supported by multiple pieces of evidence including high device reuse, concentration of IPs, abnormal refund rates, and repeated subsidy claims. However, there is uncertainty regarding logistics authenticity and specific promo cohort outliers.
